steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The sentence says Duchamp "did just that" — his sculpture accomplished exactly what he intended. The blank must signal confirmation or fulfillment of a prior claim, not similarity, contrast, or addition.

steps.title: Test each transition word
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Similarly,': Signals resemblance between two things — but the sentence is confirming one thing, not comparing two. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Indeed,': Signals confirmation or strong affirmation of the previous idea — exactly right when an intention is borne out in practice. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Instead,': Signals contrast or substitution — the opposite of confirmation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In addition,': Signals that new information is being added — does not convey that the intention was realized. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
